description: Propose recommendations for the seeded SP revenue drop. Inputs are the Anomaly (from seededToAnomaly) + the hand-crafted reference Diagnosis from eval/fixtures/reference-diagnoses-as-input.json — same Path-C-equivalent shape PR F's recommendation eval uses. PR F established this fixture passes the rubric at 5/5 (100%); regression check is for STABILITY of the recommendation thrust.

anomaly.impact: revenue for state:SP fell ~30% in a discrete window. Investigate the root cause.

input.notes: Diagnosis is loaded by id at run time from the shared fixture so we don't duplicate the ~1KB diagnosis text here. The Diagnosis JSON used is the same one PR F feeds to its recommendation eval.

golden_output.title: Re-engagement campaign targeting lapsed SP buyers from the drop week
golden_output.rationale: Order count in SP fell ~25% in week 4 (Dec 22–29), with ~4,200 affected customers going quiet. Since AOV held flat, these are full-value buyers who simply didn't purchase — not price-sensitive defectors. A targeted re-engagement broadcast with a soft incentive (e.g. free shipping or small voucher) can recover a portion of this volume before the lapse solidifies into churn.
steps: Define the target audience: SP-state customers who had purchased in the 8 weeks prior to Dec 22 but placed zero orders Dec 22–Dec 29 (approx. 4,200 customers).
Create a broadcast email campaign with subject line referencing local relevance (e.g. 'We missed you in São Paulo — here's something for the new year').
Include a soft CTA back to their most recently browsed or purchased category (electronics, fashion, or home_decor — all affected equally).
Attach a time-limited voucher (10% off or free shipping, valid 7 days) to drive urgency.
Schedule send for Jan 2–3 to catch post-holiday intent, then measure SP order count vs the same cohort in the prior 4-week baseline.

estimatedImpact.assumption: Assumes 10–20% reactivation of 4,200 lapsed SP buyers at an implied AOV of ~R$770 (~$154 USD at 5:1 BRL/USD). Low end: 420 reactivated × $50 margin-adjusted contribution; high end: 840 reactivated × $51.

prerequisites.label: Email channel active with SP customer base addressable
Voucher pool for 10% off or free-shipping incentive
Customer property 'state' populated for SP filtering

golden_output.successMetric: SP weekly order count returns to ≥95 orders (baseline avg) within 7 days of send, up from 76 in the anomaly week. Secondary: SP revenue recovers to ≥R$4.2M vs R$3.24M anomaly-week actual.

golden_output.title: Automated win-back scenario for SP buyers who miss 2+ consecutive weeks
golden_output.rationale: The SP drop is volume-driven and broad across categories — a pattern consistent with a temporary local demand or fulfillment shock that may recur (holidays, logistics disruption). Rather than a one-off fix, an automated triggered flow that detects SP customers going silent for 14+ days and sends a win-back sequence will provide durable recovery infrastructure beyond this single event.
steps: Create a triggered scenario with entry condition: customer property state = 'SP' AND no 'order' event in the last 14 days AND had ≥1 order in the prior 60 days.
Node 1 (Day 0): Send personalised email — 'We haven't seen you lately' — featuring top-selling items in their last purchased category.
Node 2 (Day 5, no purchase): Send follow-up with a 10% voucher attached and urgency copy ('Offer expires in 48 hours').
Node 3 (Day 7, still no purchase): Exit flow; flag customer for monthly re-engagement segment review.
Set frequency cap to ensure this scenario does not overlap with other active campaigns (check frequency policy before activation).
Monitor SP weekly order count and scenario conversion rate weekly.

estimatedImpact.range: +$18k – $38k recovered revenue per recurrence of a similar drop week
estimatedImpact.assumption: Assumes scenario catches ~3,000 of 4,200 lapsed SP buyers (those with email on file and qualifying history), with 8–15% win-back conversion at ~$154 AOV USD. Durable value compounds over multiple weeks if the local shock is seasonal.

prerequisites.label: Email channel active for triggered sends
Customer property 'state' indexed for scenario entry filter
Voucher pool for 10% off win-back incentive

golden_output.successMetric: Scenario conversion rate ≥10% (orders placed within 7 days of entry) within the first 14 days live; SP weekly order count trend reversal vs the anomaly-week low of 76 orders.

golden_output.title: A/B experiment: free-shipping vs percentage-off voucher for SP recovery
golden_output.rationale: The diagnosis shows AOV held flat during the SP drop — buyers who did purchase weren't trading down. This suggests price sensitivity is not the primary lever, but fulfillment friction (shipping cost or delivery reliability) may be. Testing free-shipping against a 10% cash discount will reveal which incentive is the true unlocker for SP demand recovery, preventing wasted margin on the wrong lever.
steps: Split the 4,200 lapsed SP buyers randomly into three groups: (A) free-shipping voucher, (B) 10% discount voucher, (C) control — no voucher, standard re-engagement email only.
Send all three groups the same re-engagement email copy (from the campaign in Recommendation 1); swap only the voucher/CTA block.
Primary metric: order conversion rate per group. Secondary: revenue per recipient and AOV.
Whichever variant wins becomes the default for the automated win-back scenario (Recommendation 2).
If control group converts within 3 percentage points of best variant, drop the voucher from future sends to preserve margin.

estimatedImpact.range: Margin savings of $4k – $12k over 90 days if free-shipping outperforms % discount
estimatedImpact.assumption: Assumes free-shipping cost (~R$15/order) is 3–5× cheaper than a 10% discount on an R$770 AOV order (~R$77). If free-shipping converts equally well, ~840 future win-back orders save ~R$52/order in avoided discount = ~R$44k (~$8.8k USD) per similar-scale recovery event.

prerequisites.label: Two separate voucher pools (free-shipping + 10% off) created
A/B split capability on campaign audience
SP customer segment (4,200 lapsed buyers) defined and exportable

golden_output.successMetric: Statistically significant difference (p < 0.05) in 7-day conversion rate between variant A, B, and control — with winner identified by Jan 10 and fed into the win-back scenario setup.

scoring_config.notes: Recommendations output is an array; we require the first recommendation to carry the canonical fields (lib/mcp/types.ts Recommendation). If the agent emits 0 recommendations the structural diff catches it via the missing 0.title path. The similarity judge anchors on the recommendation thrust (set of bloomreachFeatures + targeted segment).
